title: From Packing List to Trackside Operations
subtitle: What started as a drift-day packing list became a repeatable way to prepare better and carry lessons into the next event.
summary: A firsthand field guide to planning tires, vehicle support, paddock logistics, weather protection, cleanup, communication, and recovery for a complete drift day.
excerpt: My drift-day packing list did not come together all at once. It grew through repeated events, when small oversights between sessions became harder to ignore and the difference between being prepared and overpacking became clearer.

title: Beyond the Scrolling Résumé
subtitle: How I turned a long, card-based portfolio into a clearer system built around what each visitor wants to find.
summary: How I turned a long, card-based portfolio into a clearer system built around what each visitor wants to find.
excerpt: The first version of my portfolio worked well enough to publish. It showed me that the main problem was not missing content. The harder problem was deciding what a visitor should see first and how quickly they could reach the rest.

title: Performance Starts Before the Start Line
subtitle: A fast run depends on preparation that happens hours and days earlier, when fatigue or rushed decisions can hide practiced skill.
summary: A practical field note on reducing avoidable performance loss by preparing the driver as deliberately as the vehicle.
excerpt: A driver can arrive with a prepared car and still perform below their ability. Sleep, nutrition, movement, warm-up, and mental preparation influence consistency long before the first competitive run begins.

title: When AI Starts Competing for Attention
subtitle: As access becomes cheaper, entertainment shifts from watching finished media toward shaping digital and physical outcomes.
summary: As access becomes cheaper, entertainment shifts from watching finished media toward shaping digital and physical outcomes.
excerpt: An hour that once went to a television show or game can now disappear into an AI session. The change is not that AI is automatically more entertaining. It is that creating something has become easy enough to enter casually.
